stream_samples.text: doordash routed me through the bay bridge $7 toll for a $4.50 delivery. dispatch ETA was 18min via the toll vs 22min via the free bridge. who picked this
Order said 25-35 min ETA, took 78. App kept resetting the timer every time the dasher's location updated. The ETA model is broken.
[#88714 P1] Contactless drop-off photo upload fails silently on Android 14 — dasher hits submit, app says delivered, photo never reaches the customer
DashPass auto-renewed at $9.99 but my account shows the $4.99 student rate I signed up at. Support says "contact billing" and billing says "contact support".
Dispatch sent me 11 miles for a $3 base pay order through two toll bridges. App calculated tolls as $0 in the offer screen.
Refund denied for a wrong order — dasher delivered to the right address but it was someone else's food. Photo proves it. CS keeps citing "address verified".
@doordash my chipotle order arrived with the wrong protein and your refund bot offered me $3 in credit on a $19 order
Dispatch routing engine cost-of-toll weighting was zeroed out in the 2026.04.12 deploy — fixing it brings ETA accuracy down 4-6% but driver cost up ~$0.18/order
Half the restaurants in my area show "sold out" on items that the restaurant has in stock. Menu sync is days behind apparently.
[dispatch-router] toll cost feature dropped from routing model post-MLflow rollback, dasher offers no longer reflect true cost-to-serve
Contactless drop-off photo failure rate up to 8.4% on Android 14 — uploader thread killed when app backgrounded mid-S3 PUT, no retry queue
doordash showed me my driver's actual route on a map for the first time and the eta was dead-on. nice update
When the restaurant menu shows an item as available but the kitchen is out, who eats the cancellation fee? Help center is contradictory.
Dasher app crashed after I marked delivered, lost the tip + photo confirmation. This is the third week in a row.
DashPass renewal charged me twice on the same day. Support says one will reverse "in 5-7 business days". Why did it charge twice in the first place.

ETA accuracy regression (timer resets on dasher pings)

Restaurant menu sync delays ("sold out" false positives)

top_epic.title: Restore toll-cost weighting in Dispatch routing model
top_epic.rationale: 2,890 signals — heavily weighted toward dasher complaints in toll-heavy metros (SF Bay, NYC, Chicago) — bisect cleanly to deploy 2026.04.12, when an MLflow rollback dropped the toll-cost feature from the routing model. Offers now show $0 toll cost and dispatch is sending dashers through paid bridges for $3 base pay. The trade-off is an estimated 4-6% ETA-accuracy hit and ~$0.18/order in additional dispatch cost, but pay-transparency law exposure is the real risk.
top_epic.verdict: Ship-critical — dasher-pay transparency is regulator-watched and the model is currently underpaying.
top_epic.recommendation: Reinstate the toll-cost feature with a fresh training pass, gate dispatch behind toll-aware offers in the top 12 metros, and ship a dasher-facing toll-cost preview in the offer card.
acceptance_criteria: Toll-cost feature reinstated in dispatch model with parity to pre-04.12 baseline
Offer card shows estimated toll cost per route with <=$0.10 P90 error
Dasher pay-per-mile post-toll meets policy floor in top 12 toll-heavy metros
Dispatch routing decision logs include toll feature value for every offer
